title: 10 best herbal tinctures for nausea
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for nausea are concentrated liquid extracts of plants that are used to alleviate symptoms of nausea. They are a popular natural remedy for nausea because they are non-habit forming and have fewer side effects compared to over-the-counter medications. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with nausea include ginger, peppermint, and chamomile. Ginger has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, while peppermint and chamomile have calming effects on the stomach and digestive system. By using herbal tinctures, individuals can find relief from nausea and improve their quality of life, especially those undergoing chemotherapy or recovering from surgery.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tincture helps with nausea because it contains active compounds called gingerols and shogaols, which have been shown to alleviate digestive issues and reduce feelings of queasiness. These compounds work by blocking the serotonin receptors in the gut that trigger nausea and vomiting. Additionally, ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an irritated stomach lining, further reducing nausea symptoms. Its rapid absorption into the bloodstream allows for quick relief, making it a popular natural remedy for nausea caused by various factors such as motion sickness, chemotherapy, or pregnancy.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Ginger tincture can help reduce the sensation of nausea and prevent vomiting by blocking the serotonin receptors in the gut, thanks to its active constituents, gingerols and shogaols.
Gastroprotective: Ginger tincture helps protect the stomach lining from irritation and inflammation, reducing nausea caused by gastric disturbances, due to the presence of gingerols and zingerone.

Prokinetic: Ginger tincture can stimulate gastric motility, promoting the faster movement of food from the stomach to the intestines, alleviating nausea caused by delayed gastric emptying, owing to the action of gingerols and shogaols.

Analgesic: Ginger tincture has pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate nausea caused by abdominal pain or discomfort, as its active constituents, gingerols and shogaols, inhibit the production of prostaglandins and leukotrienes.

Anxiolytic: Ginger tincture may help reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to nausea symptoms, by modulating neurotransmitter activity in the brain, particularly GABA, serotonin, and dopamine, due to the presence of gingerols and shogaols.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes are commonly used to make medicinal tea for nausea due to their potent anti-emetic properties.
Roots: Ginger roots contain bioactive compounds that help alleviate nausea and vomiting, making them an excellent choice for herbal teas.
Seeds: Ginger seeds have been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues such as nausea, as they contain compounds that promote gastrointestinal motility.
Flowers: Ginger flowers can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea symptoms, as they contain volatile oils that have carminative and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh ginger root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or rum over the ginger, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Secure a lid on the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, discarding the ginger solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle and use 1-2 mL as needed for nausea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tincture helps with nausea because it contains menthol and methyl salicylate, compounds that have antispasmodic and calming effects on the gastrointestinal tract. These substances can help relax the smooth muscles of the stomach and intestines, reducing feelings of queasiness and promoting normal digestion. Additionally, peppermint has been shown to improve the flow of bile, which can help break down fats and speed up the digestion process, further alleviating nausea. Overall, peppermint tincture can provide fast-acting relief for nausea, making it a popular natural remedy for those seeking a gentle and effective way to soothe an upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic: Peppermint tincture helps reduce nausea and vomiting by stimulating the release of digestive enzymes, thanks to menthol and menthone, its main active constituents.

Carminative: It helps alleviate symptoms of nausea caused by gas and bloating in the digestive tract, due to its ability to relax gastrointestinal muscles, which is attributed to menthol and menthyl acetate.
Anesthetic: Peppermint tincture has a local anesthetic effect that can help relieve the discomfort and pain associated with nausea, as a result of menthol's ability to desensitize nerve endings.
Antispasmodic: Peppermint tincture can help soothe an upset stomach by reducing muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.
Choleretic: Peppermint tincture promotes bile secretion, which can help speed up digestion and reduce nausea, thanks to the active constituents rosmarinic acid and menthol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the peppermint plant are commonly used in teas to alleviate nausea due to their calming and antispasmodic properties.
Flowers: Peppermint flowers are used in medicinal tea for nausea because they contain menthol, which helps relax the gastrointestinal tract and alleviate symptoms.
Seeds: Peppermint seeds are rich in essential oils, making them a valuable addition to teas aimed at reducing nausea and promoting digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves, filling a clean, dry jar halfway, ensuring they're loosely packed.
Pour 100-proof vodka over the leaves until they are completely submerged, covering the jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out the liquid from the leaves.
Label the bottle with the contents and date, storing it in a cool, dark place for up to a year, using 1-2 mL as needed for nausea relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort tincture helps with nausea because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that aid in soothing the digestive tract and alleviating symptoms of nausea. The active compounds in mugwort, such as thujone and camphor, work together to regulate the digestive system and promote the production of digestive juices, which can help break down food and reduce feelings of queasiness. Furthermore, mugwort has been shown to stimulate the release of dopamine, a neurotransmitter that plays a role in regulating nausea and vomiting, making it an effective natural remedy for managing nausea symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic property: Mugwort tincture helps reduce nausea and vomiting by stimulating the digestive system and promoting the secretion of digestive juices, thanks to its active constituents like volatile oils, flavonoids, and tannins.

Carminative property: Mugwort tincture aids in expelling gas from the stomach and intestines, which can help alleviate nausea caused by bloating or indigestion, due to its active constituents, such as volatile oils like thujone and camphor.

Spasmolytic property: Mugwort tincture helps relax smooth muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can lead to nausea, attributed to its active constituents like flavonoids and essential oils.

Nervine property: Mugwort tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress-related nausea, due to its active constituents like volatile oils, flavonoids, and alkaloids.

Stomachic property: Mugwort tincture can help stimulate the appetite and promote the health of the stomach lining, which can be beneficial for nausea caused by a lack of appetite or stomach irritation, thanks to its active constituents like tannins and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain volatile oils and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and relax gastrointestinal muscles, alleviating nausea.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, helping to soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea symptoms.
Stems: Contain bitter compounds that can stimulate digestion, bile production, and appetite, thereby reducing nausea.
Rhizomes: Packed with therapeutic constituents like essential oils, flavonoids, and alkaloids, helping to manage gastrointestinal discomfort and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried mugwort herb and place it in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid.
Store the final tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle and use 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for nausea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tincture helps with nausea because it contains volatile oils, such as anethole, fenchone, and estragole, which have antispasmodic and carminative properties. These compounds help relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing smooth muscle spasms and gas buildup. Fennel tincture can also stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, promoting healthy digestion and easing feelings of queasiness. Its soothing effects on the stomach lining make it an effective remedy for nausea, whether it's caused by motion sickness, pregnancy, or gastrointestinal disorders.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic property: Fennel tincture can help reduce nausea and vomiting by relaxing the gastrointestinal muscles, which is important for relieving the discomfort and potential dehydration caused by nausea. This property is attributed to the active constituents, such as anethole and fenchone.

Carminative property: Fennel tincture aids in expelling gas from the stomach and intestines, reducing bloating and cramping. This is crucial for alleviating nausea, as bloating and cramping can exacerbate the sensation of nausea. The carminative effect is primarily due to the presence of volatile oils, such as anethole and fenchone.

Spasmolytic property: Fennel tincture helps relieve smooth muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, which can contribute to nausea. By reducing spasms, fennel tincture can help alleviate nausea symptoms. This property is attributed to the active constituents, such as estragole and limonene.

Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel tincture can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can cause or worsen nausea. This is important for managing nausea, as inflammation can lead to irritation and discomfort in the stomach and intestines. The anti-inflammatory effect is primarily due to the presence of volatile oils, such as anethole and fenchone.

Cholagogue property: Fennel tincture promotes the production and flow of bile from the liver and gallbladder, which can help improve digestion and reduce nausea. This property is attributed to the active constituents, such as anethole and fenchone. By improving digestion, fennel tincture can help alleviate nausea caused by poor digestion or indigestion.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in volatile oils, including anethole, which helps to relax gastrointestinal spasms, reducing nausea and promoting healthy digestion.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant contain flavonoids and antioxidants that help soothe the stomach lining, alleviating nausea and aiding in digestion.
Rhyzomes: Fennel rhyzomes have a high concentration of essential oils, which can help regulate digestion, reduce bloating, and alleviate nausea.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of fresh fennel leaves and roots, placing them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 cup of vodka or rum with at least 40% alcohol content over the chopped fennel, ensuring complete coverage.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid and discarding the solid plant material.
Store the resulting fennel tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year. For nausea relief, take 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) of the tincture as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway tincture helps with nausea because it contains anti-spasmodic and carminative properties that aid in soothing the digestive system and reducing bloating. The active compounds in caraway, such as carvol and carvone, work together to relax the gastrointestinal muscles and alleviate discomfort. This herbal remedy has been traditionally used to treat various gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, indigestion, and stomach cramps. Caraway tincture provides a natural and effective way to manage nausea, making it an ideal choice for those seeking relief without relying on pharmaceutical interventions.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic property: Caraway tincture's antiemetic property helps reduce nausea and vomiting by relaxing the gastrointestinal muscles, which is important for relieving the discomfort and distress caused by nausea. This property is attributed to the presence of carvol and limonene in caraway seeds.

Carminative property: Caraway tincture's carminative property helps alleviate nausea by reducing bloating, gas, and stomach cramps, which can contribute to feelings of queasiness. The active constituents responsible for this property are carvone and limonene.

Spasmolytic property: Caraway tincture's spasmolytic property helps soothe the stomach and intestinal muscles, reducing muscle spasms that can cause nausea. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils in caraway seeds.

Anxiolytic property: Caraway tincture's anxiolytic property helps alleviate nausea by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which can help reduce feelings of queasiness. This property is attributed to the presence of volatile oils and flavonoids in caraway seeds.

Digestive stimulant property: Caraway tincture's digestive stimulant property helps improve digestion by increasing the production of digestive enzymes, which can help reduce nausea caused by indigestion. This property is due to the presence of carvone and limonene in caraway seeds.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used in making medicinal tea for nausea due to their carminative properties that help to relieve bloating, gas, and digestive discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves of the caraway tincture plant are used in tea preparation for nausea, as they contain anti-emetic compounds that help to soothe the stomach and alleviate feelings of nausea.

Fruits: The fruits of the caraway tincture plant, also known as meridian fennel, are often used in tea for nausea due to their volatile oils and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce nausea symptoms.

Rhizomes: Caraway rhizomes are used in medicinal tea for nausea due to their ability to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help to improve digestion and reduce feelings of nausea.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried caraway seeds and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of 100-proof vodka or rum over the chopped seeds, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL as needed for nausea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint tincture helps with nausea because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe an upset stomach and relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing the likelihood of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, spearmint has been shown to promote the production of bile, which can aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of nausea. Its refreshing and calming aroma can also help to reduce feelings of anxiety and stress, which can contribute to digestive discomfort. Overall, spearmint tincture offers a natural and effective way to manage nausea and support a healthy digestive system.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Spearmint tincture can help reduce nausea and vomiting by blocking the action of neurotransmitters that stimulate the vomiting response, thanks to its active constituents like menthol and limonene.

Carminative: Spearmint tincture can help relieve gas and bloating, which can contribute to nausea, due to its ability to relax gastrointestinal muscles and reduce spasms, thanks to its active constituents like menthol and rosmarinic acid.

Antispasmodic: Spearmint tincture can help reduce intestinal spasms and cramping, which can cause nausea, due to its ability to relax smooth muscles and reduce inflammation, thanks to its active constituents like menthol and rosmarinic acid.

Anxiolytic: Spearmint tincture can help reduce anxiety and stress, which can trigger nausea, due to its calming and soothing effects on the nervous system, thanks to its active constituents like menthol and rosmarinic acid.

Digestive stimulant: Spearmint tincture can help improve digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can help reduce nausea, due to its ability to increase the production of digestive enzymes and bile, thanks to its active constituents like menthol and carvone.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and volatile oils, which can help regulate digestion and reduce feelings of nausea.
Seeds: High in essential oils and fatty acids, providing anti-emetic properties that can help control nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of fresh spearmint leaves and stems, place them in a clean glass jar, and pour 1 cup of vodka or rum with at least 40% alcohol content.
Make sure the herbs are fully submerged, cover the jar with a tight-fitting lid, and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks.
Shake the jar gently every few days to help the extraction process.
After the specified time, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid and discarding the plant material.
Store the resulting spearmint tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle, and take 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) up to three times a day as needed for nausea relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with nausea because it contains active compounds, such as bisabolol and chamazulene, that have anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and calming properties. These compounds work together to soothe the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract. Moreover, chamomile has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help break down food and reduce feelings of nausea. Its mild sedative effect can also help relax the mind and body, further easing the sensation of nausea. As a result, german chamomile tincture is a popular natural remedy for managing nausea and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: German chamomile tincture can help reduce nausea and vomiting due to its anti-emetic properties, attributed to active constituents like chamazulene and bisabolol.

Carminative: Its carminative properties aid in expelling gas from the stomach and intestines, alleviating bloating and discomfort that can exacerbate nausea, thanks to volatile oils like alpha-bisabolol and matricin.
Spasmolytic: German chamomile tincture helps relax gastrointestinal muscles, reducing spasms and cramping that can trigger nausea, owing to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids.
Anxiolytic: The tincture can help manage nausea caused by anxiety or stress, as it has anxiolytic effects, attributed to apigenin and other flavonoids that bind to benzodiazepine receptors.

Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe irritated gastrointestinal tissues, reducing nausea, thanks to the action of chamazulene, bisabolol, and other active constituents.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in volatile oils and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in alleviating nausea and promoting digestion.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help soothe the stomach lining, reducing nausea and vomiting sensations.
Seeds: German chamomile seeds are a source of essential oils, which can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, thereby easing nausea and aiding in digestion.
remedy_recipe: Fill a clean jar with 2 parts fresh or 1 part dried German chamomile flowers, then add 1 part vodka or rum (80-100 proof) to cover the flowers.
Tightly seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the recommended time, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solid plant material.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, label it with the name, date, and potency, and store it in a cool, dark place.
Take 1-2 mL of the tincture up to three times a day as needed for nausea, or consult a healthcare professional for personalized dosage recommendations.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tincture helps with nausea because it contains antispasmodic and carminative properties that can aid in calming the digestive system and alleviating symptoms of nausea. The soothing effects of lemon balm work to reduce muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from discomfort and queasiness. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to promote the production of bile in the liver, which can help improve overall digestion and reduce feelings of nausea. Its refreshing and uplifting aroma can also help to alleviate stress and anxiety, which can contribute to digestive issues and nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic property: Lemon balm tincture's anti-emetic property helps reduce nausea and vomiting by blocking the actions of chemicals that stimulate the vomiting response, thanks to its active constituents such as rosmarinic acid and citronellal.

Carminative property: This herb's carminative property aids in expelling excess gas from the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating one of the common causes of nausea, due to the presence of volatile oils like citral and geraniol.

Anxiolytic property: Lemon balm tincture's anxiolytic effect helps reduce anxiety and stress, which can often trigger nausea, by interacting with the GABA receptors in the brain, with rosmarinic acid and eugenol being the key compounds.

Spasmolytic property: The spasmolytic property of lemon balm helps relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, thus alleviating nausea caused by spasms or cramps, owing to the action of compounds like eugenol and tannins.

Antimicrobial property: Lemon balm tincture's antimicrobial property helps combat nausea caused by gastrointestinal infections by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses, attributed mainly to the presence of citral and citronellal.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in teas for nausea due to their calming and soothing properties.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm are used in teas for nausea as they contain volatile oils that can help relax the digestive system.
Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, the stems of lemon balm can still contribute to the tea's effectiveness in alleviating nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh lemon balm leaves, chop finely, and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 cup of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the leaves, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle, label with the ingredients and date, and take 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for nausea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marjoram tincture helps with nausea because it contains anti-spasmodic and carminative properties that aid in soothing the digestive system and alleviating symptoms of nausea. The volatile oils found in marjoram, such as terpinen-4-ol and γ-terpinene, work to reduce inflammation and relax the gastrointestinal muscles, which can help to relieve feelings of nausea and promote healthy digestion. Additionally, marjoram has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that may contribute to nausea. Overall, marjoram tincture is a natural and effective remedy for managing nausea and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic: Marjoram tincture helps reduce nausea and vomiting by containing antiemetic compounds like terpinen-4-ol and carvacrol.
Carminative: Its ability to relieve gas and bloating, common causes of nausea, is due to the presence of volatile oils such as sabinene, alpha- and gamma-terpinene, and cis-sabinene hydrate.
Spasmolytic: Marjoram tincture relaxes gastrointestinal smooth muscles, alleviating nausea caused by spasms or cramps, thanks to its content of flavonoids and monoterpenes.
Anti-inflammatory: The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to its rosmarinic acid and triterpene content, help soothe irritated gastrointestinal tissues, reducing nausea.
Anxiolytic: Marjoram tincture can help reduce nausea caused by anxiety or stress, as it contains calming compounds like linalool and cis-jasmone, which promote relaxation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of marjoram tincture plant are rich in antispasmodic and carminative properties which help in reducing nausea and soothing the stomach.
Flowers: The flowers of marjoram tincture plant contain calming and soothing properties which help in alleviating nausea and promoting relaxation.
Seeds: The seeds of marjoram tincture plant are known for their antiemetic properties, making them useful in treating nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried marjoram leaves and stems, placing them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the marjoram solids.
Store the tincture in a tightly capped, dark glass bottle, keeping it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. For nausea, take 1-2 ml three times a day or as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley tincture helps with nausea because it contains anti-emetic properties that aid in reducing feelings of queasiness and vomiting. The active compounds in parsley, such as apiol and myristicin, work together to soothe the digestive system and alleviate discomfort. Additionally, parsley's calming effects on the stomach lining can help to relax muscles, further minimizing the sensations of nausea. Its natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties contribute to its effectiveness in managing nausea, making parsley tincture a valuable herbal remedy for promoting digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Parsley tincture can help reduce nausea and vomiting by blocking the signals that trigger these symptoms, due to the presence of active constituents such as apiol and myristicin.
Carminative: Parsley tincture can help alleviate nausea by reducing gas and bloating in the digestive tract, thanks to its content of volatile oils like limonene and alpha-pinene.

Antispasmodic: Parsley tincture can help soothe muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, which can contribute to nausea, due to the presence of anti-spasmodic compounds like eugenol and myristicin.

Diuretic: Parsley tincture can help reduce bloating and water retention, which can alleviate nausea, by promoting the production of urine and the elimination of excess fluids, due to its active constituents like apiol and asarone.

Vitamin and mineral-rich: Parsley tincture can help replenish essential nutrients, such as vitamins A, C, and K, and minerals like potassium and iron, which can be depleted due to nausea and vomiting, contributing to overall well-being and improved digestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain volatile oils and flavonoids that can help reduce nausea and vomiting.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and have anti-emetic properties that can help alleviate nausea.
Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe the digestive system and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of fresh parsley leaves and stems, place them in a clean glass jar, then add 1 cup of vodka (80 proof) or grain alcohol (95%).
Submerge the parsley completely, cover the jar with a tight-fitting lid, and let it steep for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking it gently every few days.
After the steeping period, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer into a dark glass bottle or jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the parsley solids.
Discard the parsley solids and store the liquid in a cool, dark place; the tincture will last for several years.
Take 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) of the parsley tincture, 3 times a day, as needed for nausea, or as recommended by a healthcare practitioner.
